plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the single name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for greater income and stability, favoring a conservative approach.
Trim QQQ to 15% to balance broad index exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 10% to lower single name concentration.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month outlook, prioritizing capital preservation and minimizing drawdown. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but potential upside is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQ to 15% to balance broad index exposure.
Buy AAPL at 10% to add a high-quality tech name with a strong scanner read.
Buy AVGO at 8% to add another high-quality chip name.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer, keeping total adds within limits.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5%/month growth, focusing on risk-first growth by reducing leverage and diversifying into quality tech. It balances potential returns with a controlled level of risk, aiming for smoother equity curve growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all QQQI to reallocate capital towards higher growth opportunities.
Trim QQQ to 10% to further concentrate into high-beta names.
Add SOXL to 10% to increase leveraged semiconductor exposure, accepting higher risk.
Buy LEU at 10% to capitalize on a top-scoring scanner setup.
Buy GUSH at 10% to add a high risk/reward setup, leveraging strong scanner signals.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month growth, leaning into leverage and concentration for stretched growth potential. This approach significantly increases both upside and drawdown risk, with leveraged ETFs subject to daily decay and rebalancing, requiring strict stop discipline to manage potential rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the single name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with income focus.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize exposure to income-generating ETFs.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth-oriented exposure.
Sell CRWD to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer, prioritizing low drawdown and reliable distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets a ~1%/month outlook, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It emphasizes income-generating ETFs and broad index exposure, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in market selloffs.
